headline: What Tyler Technologies (TYL)'s New US$200 Million 10b5-1 Buyback Plan Means For Shareholders
summary: In March 2026, Tyler Technologies adopted a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan to repurchase up to US$200 million of its common stock between March 16 and April 30, under a refreshed US$1.00 billion buyback authorization approved in February. This move highlights Tyler’s ongoing use of structured share repurchases as a capital allocation tool, drawing on cash and its credit facility to potentially enhance per-share metrics while maintaining flexibility. We’ll now examine how this new US$200 million...
